questions.objective_ref: Define automation bias in plain terms and explain why it is a cognitive bias rather than a deliberate choice.
Distinguish between an omission error and a commission error in the context of automation bias.
Explain the two main cognitive mechanisms — cognitive offloading and perceived reliability — that cause automation bias to occur.
Identify automation bias in real-world examples drawn from aviation, medicine, and AI-assisted tools.
Recognise how System 1 thinking (from 9.1) amplifies automation bias when an automated recommendation is presented without explanation.
Describe at least two practical steps an individual or organisation can take to reduce the risk of automation bias.
